title: Review and text production
description: A2 French for English speakers: a consolidating review of all key A2 grammar structures (passe compose vs imparfait, futur, conditionnel, y/en, negation, conjunctions) and a practical guide to writing short postcards, messages, and emails about past events and future plans. Explained in English.

cards.front: Tableau de bord A2 : structures clés
cards.back: A2 grammar dashboard: passe compose, imparfait, futur simple, conditionnel, y/en, negation, conjunctions
cards.notes:
A quick reference for the seven A2 grammar pillars you have studied:

| Structure | Key use | Quick example |
|---|---|---|
| **Passe compose** | completed past action | *J'ai mangé / Je suis allé(e)* |
| **Imparfait** | background / habit in past | *Il faisait beau / Je travaillais* |
| **Futur simple** | future plans/predictions | *Nous irons en France* |
| **Conditionnel** | polite request / hypothesis | *Je voudrais un café* |
| **y / en** | replace à+place/thing and de+thing | *J'y vais / J'en prends* |
| **Negation** | ne...plus/jamais/rien/personne | *Je ne sais rien* |
| **Conjunctions** | link clauses | *parce que, donc, bien que* |

cards.front: Il faisait froid quand je suis sorti(e).
cards.back: Imparfait (background) + passe compose (event) in one sentence
cards.notes:
The classic contrast: **imparfait** sets the scene or background (*faisait* = it was cold — a state), **passe compose** describes the event that happened (*je suis sorti(e)* = I went out — a completed action).

- *Je lisais quand le telephone a sonne.* (I was reading when the phone rang.)
- *Elle habitait a Lyon quand elle a rencontre Marc.* (She was living in Lyon when she met Marc.)

cards.front: Si j'ai le temps, j'irai au marche. / Je voudrais partir tot.
cards.back: Futur simple for real future plans; conditionnel for polite requests and hypotheses
cards.notes:
**Futur simple**: regular verbs add endings *-ai, -as, -a, -ons, -ez, -ont* to the infinitive. Use for firm future plans or predictions.

**Conditionnel**: same stem as futur + imperfect endings *-ais, -ais, -ait, -ions, -iez, -aient*. Use for:
- Polite requests: *Je voudrais..., Pourriez-vous...?*
- Hypotheses (si + imparfait): *Si j'avais de l'argent, je voyagerais.*
cards.hint: Futur = definite future. Conditionnel = polite or hypothetical.

cards.front: J'y suis alle(e). / J'en ai achete trois.
cards.back: y (replaces a + place/thing) and en (replaces de + thing / partitive) before the verb
cards.notes:
**y** = replaces *a + place/thing*: *Tu vas au marche ? J'y vais.* (Are you going to the market? I'm going there.)

**en** = replaces *de + thing* or a partitive/quantity: *Tu veux du fromage ? J'en veux.* / *Il en a acheté trois.*

Both go **before the conjugated verb** (or auxiliary in passe compose): *Je n'y suis pas allé. / Je n'en ai pas pris.*

Four key negatives beyond *ne...pas*:

| Negative | Meaning | PC placement |
|---|---|---|
| ne...plus | not anymore | between aux + participle |
| ne...jamais | never | between aux + participle |
| ne...rien | nothing | between aux + participle |
| ne...personne | nobody | **after** participle |

Examples: *Je n'ai plus d'argent. / Je n'ai jamais menti. / Je n'ai rien vu. / Je n'ai vu personne.*

cards.front: parce que / donc / mais / bien que / quand / si
cards.back: because / so (therefore) / but / although / when / if
cards.notes:
Key conjunctions for building longer sentences:

- **parce que** (because) — gives a reason: *Je reste parce qu'il pleut.*
- **donc** (so, therefore) — gives a consequence: *Il fait froid, donc je mets un manteau.*
- **mais** (but) — contrasts: *Je veux venir, mais je suis fatigue(e).*
- **bien que** + subjunctive (although) — concedes: *Bien qu'il fasse froid, je sors.*
- **quand** (when) + indicative: *Quand j'arrive, j'appelle.*
- **si** (if) + present → futur: *Si tu viens, nous mangerons ensemble.*

cards.front: Cher Paul, / Chere Marie, ... Bisous, / A bientot,
cards.back: postcard/message opening: Cher (m.) / Chere (f.) + name; closing: Bisous / A bientot / Bien a toi
cards.notes:
## How to open and close a French postcard or informal message

**Openings:**
- *Cher Paul,* (Dear Paul — to a man)
- *Chere Marie,* (Dear Marie — to a woman)
- *Bonjour a tous !* (Hello everyone!)

**Closings:**
- *Bisous,* (Kisses — friendly/family)
- *A bientot,* (See you soon)
- *Bien a toi,* (Warmly yours)
- *Grosses bises,* (Big kisses — very warm)

**Note:** *Cher / Chere* follows the same masculine/feminine pattern as adjectives.

cards.back: First... Then... Finally... (sequence connectors for postcards and stories)
cards.notes:
Use these connectors to give your text a clear flow when describing a sequence of events:

- **D'abord** — First of all
- **Ensuite** / **Puis** — Then / Next
- **Enfin** / **Finalement** — Finally / In the end
- **Apres cela** — After that
- **Le lendemain** — The next day

Example postcard body: *D'abord, nous sommes arrives a Rome. Ensuite, nous avons visite le Colisee. Enfin, nous avons dine dans un petit restaurant.*

cards.back: to wait for / bookshop / (small) change — not 'attend', 'library', 'money'
cards.notes:
Three false friends from across the A2 course:

| French word | Actual meaning | English false friend |
|---|---|---|
| **attendre** | to wait for | *attend* (= assister a) |
| **librairie** | bookshop | *library* (= bibliotheque) |
| **monnaie** | change / coins | *money* (= argent) |

Examples:
- *J'attends le bus.* (I am waiting for the bus.)
- *Je cherche une librairie.* (I am looking for a bookshop.)
- *Tu as de la monnaie ?* (Do you have change?)
cards.hint: False friends look like English words but mean something different.

cards.front: Chere Sophie, Je suis a Barcelone depuis trois jours. D'abord nous avons visite la Sagrada Familia, ensuite nous avons mange des tapas. Demain nous irons a la plage. Bisous, Emma
cards.back: model postcard: greeting + past events (passe compose) + future plan (futur simple) + closing
cards.notes:
This postcard uses:
1. **Greeting**: *Chere Sophie,*
2. **Present context**: *Je suis a Barcelone depuis trois jours.*
3. **Past events** (passe compose): *nous avons visite... nous avons mange...*
4. **Sequence connector**: *D'abord... ensuite...*
5. **Future plan** (futur simple): *nous irons a la plage.*
6. **Closing**: *Bisous, Emma*

This five-part structure works for any postcard, email, or holiday message.

cards.back: If + present tense → future simple (real condition)
cards.notes:
To talk about a **real possibility** in the future, French uses:

> **Si** + present + **futur simple**

Examples:
- *Si j'ai de l'argent, j'acheterai un nouveau velo.* (If I have money, I will buy a new bike.)
- *Si tu viens demain, nous pourrons diner ensemble.* (If you come tomorrow, we will be able to have dinner together.)

Note: **never** use futur directly after *si* for a real condition — that is an English trap.
cards.hint: si + present (NOT si + futur) → then futur simple.

cards.front: J'attends le bus depuis vingt minutes.
cards.back: I have been waiting for the bus for twenty minutes.
cards.notes: *attendre* = to wait **for** (the *for* is built in — do not add *pour*). *J'attends le bus, j'attends mon ami.* Note also: *depuis* + present tense = an action that started in the past and is still going on (English uses present perfect continuous).

steps.title: A2 in review: the key structures
steps.body:
# Review and text production

To finish A2: we review the key structures and practise using them in your own short texts.

You have covered seven grammar pillars across this course. Here they are in one place:

| Structure | Core rule | Example |
|---|---|---|
| **Passe compose** | avoir/etre + participle; etre verbs agree | *Elle est partie. / J'ai vu.* |
| **Imparfait** | background, habits, states in the past | *Il faisait beau. / Je travaillais.* |
| **PC vs imparfait** | event vs background in same sentence | *Je lisais quand il est arrive.* |
| **Futur simple** | infinitive + endings for real future | *Nous partirons demain.* |
| **Conditionnel** | polite requests, si-hypotheses | *Je voudrais... / Si j'avais..., je ferais...* |
| **y / en** | y = a+place/thing; en = de+thing/partitive | *J'y vais. / J'en veux.* |
| **Negation** | ne...plus/jamais/rien/personne | *Je ne sais rien.* |
| **Conjunctions** | parce que, donc, mais, si, quand | *Je reste parce qu'il pleut.* |

## False friends to remember

| French | Actual meaning | NOT |
|---|---|---|
| attendre | to wait for | to attend |
| librairie | bookshop | library |
| monnaie | (small) change / coins | money |

In the exercises that follow, each task mixes at least two of these topics.

steps.title: Writing a postcard or short message
steps.body:
## How to write a short postcard / message / email in French

A French postcard or informal message has five parts:

1. **Greeting** — *Cher [name],* (m.) / *Chere [name],* (f.)
2. **Opening line** — where you are or what the context is (present tense or passe compose)
3. **Body** — past events + present situation + future plans; use **connectors**
4. **Closing remark** — a brief forward-looking sentence (*A bientot !*, *On se voit la semaine prochaine.*)
5. **Sign-off** — *Bisous,* / *A bientot,* / *Bien a toi,*

## Text-building blocks

### Sequence connectors
| French | English |
|---|---|
| **D'abord** | First of all |
| **Ensuite** / **Puis** | Then / Next |
| **Apres cela** | After that |
| **Enfin** / **Finalement** | Finally |
| **Le lendemain** | The next day |

### Tense choices
- **What happened** → passe compose (*Nous avons visite, je suis alle(e)*)
- **Background / how things were** → imparfait (*Il faisait chaud, les rues etaient animees*)
- **Future plans** → futur simple (*Nous irons, je visiterai*) or aller + infinitive (*Je vais partir*)
- **Polite request / wish** → conditionnel (*Je voudrais revenir*)

## Model postcard

*Chere Sophie,*

*Je suis a Barcelone depuis trois jours. D'abord nous avons visite la Sagrada Familia, ensuite nous avons mange des tapas dans un petit restaurant. Il faisait un temps magnifique ! Demain nous irons a la plage.*

*Bisous,*
*Emma*

steps.title: Combining structures: a final checklist
steps.body:
## Putting it all together

When you write or speak at A2 level, aim to combine these patterns naturally:

### PC vs imparfait — the two-layer past
> **Imparfait** = the canvas (ongoing state / background / habit)  
> **Passe compose** = the brushstroke (specific event that happened)

*Le soleil brillait (imparfait) quand nous sommes arrives (PC).*  
(The sun was shining when we arrived.)

### Conditionnel for politeness and hypothesis
- Polite: *Pourriez-vous m'aider ?* (Could you help me?)
- Hypothesis: *Si j'avais le temps, je lirais plus.* (If I had time, I would read more.)

### y and en — avoid repetition
Do not repeat a noun you just mentioned — replace it:
- *Tu vas a la boulangerie ? Oui, j'y vais.* (Yes, I'm going there.)
- *Tu as achete des croissants ? J'en ai achete deux.* (I bought two of them.)

### Advanced negation review
| Sentence | Meaning |
|---|---|
| *Je ne mange plus de viande.* | I no longer eat meat. |
| *Je n'ai jamais vu la mer.* | I have never seen the sea. |
| *Je n'ai rien compris.* | I understood nothing. |
| *Je n'ai vu personne.* | I saw nobody. |

**Remember**: *personne* goes **after** the participle; the others go before.

### Quick false-friends recap
- *J'attends le bus.* (I am waiting **for** the bus — not *j'attends pour*)
- *Je cherche une librairie.* (I'm looking for a **bookshop**, not a library)
- *Tu as de la monnaie ?* (Do you have **change**, not just money in general)

steps.title: Match the tense to its use

exercise.prompt: Match each French sentence to the description of what its tense expresses.

pairs.left: Je voudrais une chambre, s'il vous plait.

steps.title: Write the opening of a postcard to a female friend named Claire

exercise.prompt: Write the greeting line for a postcard addressed to a female friend called Claire. (Use *Chere* for a female recipient.)
exercise.hint: Cher = masculine, Chere = feminine. Use the feminine form here.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the correct past tense: *Il faisait beau quand nous ___ (arriver).*  Type the passe compose form of *arriver* (nous, masculine group).
exercise.sentence: Il faisait beau quand nous ___ au bord de la mer.
exercise.hint: arriver takes etre; nous (mixed group) → arrivés.

exercise.prompt: Your friend asks: *Tu vas au supermarche ?* Reply: *Yes, I'm going there.* Use the pronoun **y**.
exercise.hint: y replaces a + place. J'y vais = I'm going there.

exercise.prompt: Arrange these words to make a sentence for a postcard: *First, we visited the Eiffel Tower.*
exercise.hint: Use d'abord (first) + passe compose (nous avons visite).

steps.title: False friend: which sentence is correct?

exercise.prompt: You want to say: *I am looking for a bookshop.* Which French sentence is correct?

steps.title: Advanced negation in the passe compose

exercise.prompt: Translate: *I never went to Italy.* (Use ne...jamais and the passe compose of *aller*.)
accept: Je ne suis jamais alle en Italie.
Je ne suis jamais allee en Italie.
je ne suis jamais alle en Italie.
Je ne suis jamais allé en Italie.
Je ne suis jamais allée en Italie.
exercise.hint: aller takes etre; jamais goes between the auxiliary and the participle: je ne suis jamais alle(e).
distractors: Je n'ai jamais alle en Italie.
Je suis jamais alle en Italie.
Je ne suis pas jamais alle en Italie.

exercise.prompt: Match each French word to its correct English meaning.
Fill in the futur simple of *pouvoir* (nous) to complete the *si* sentence: *Si tu viens demain, nous ___ diner ensemble.*
exercise.sentence: Si tu viens demain, nous ___ diner ensemble.

exercise.prompt: Arrange these words: *Tomorrow we will go to the beach.* (futur simple of aller: nous irons)
exercise.hint: Futur simple of aller: nous irons (not nous allerons — aller is irregular in the futur).

steps.title: Write a postcard body sentence using connectors and passe compose

exercise.prompt: Write one sentence for a postcard body using **d'abord** and the passe compose of *visiter* (nous). You visited the museum. Start with *D'abord,*
accept: D'abord, nous avons visite le musee.
D'abord nous avons visite le musee.
D'abord, nous avons visité le musée.
D'abord nous avons visité le musée.
exercise.hint: d'abord = first of all; visiter takes avoir: nous avons visite.
distractors: D'abord, nous sommes visite le musee.
Ensuite, nous avons visite le musee.
